name: memory-bootstrap
description: Initialize memory system connection at session start. Query past implementations from Neo4j before coding. Must be used for any new development work.

name: memory-query
description: Search for previous implementations, architectural decisions, and code patterns in the Neo4j knowledge graph. Use before implementing new features.

name: memory-log
description: Log implementation reflections to Neo4j after completing work. Create entities, observations, and relations for knowledge persistence.

name: memory-orchestrate
description: Coordinate the full agent surgical team. Delegate to appropriate subagents based on task type. Ensures conceptual integrity through clear interfaces.

name: memory-plan
description: Create implementation plans following Brooksian principles. Break work into atomic subtasks with clear dependencies and contracts.

name: memory-build
description: Execute delegated coding tasks with memory-aware patterns. Implements modular, functional code with strong contracts.

name: memory-test
description: Test memory system integrity and persistence. Validates PostgreSQL traces, Neo4j knowledge, and HITL gates.

name: memory-review
description: Review code for security, Steel Frame compliance, and memory best practices. Adversarial review with evidence-based feedback.

name: memory-validate
description: Validate builds, types, and memory integrity. Final checkpoint before claiming completion.

name: superpowers-memory
description: Add memory logging and hydration to Superpowers skills. Use at session start to retrieve context, at session end to log outcomes.
